name: TRUGS Language Vocabulary
description: Canonical TRL vocabulary — 190 words across 8 parts of speech. Generated from SPEC_vocabulary.md by tools/build_language_trug.py.

properties.definition: An executing unit of work with its own state
A long-lived process that accepts requests
A callable unit that accepts input and returns output
A node that performs exactly one operation
A bound entity capable of obligations and permissions
An entity authorized to act on behalf of another
The entity on whose behalf an agent acts
A typed value: input, output, or intermediate state
A named, persistent byte sequence
A single structured data item with named fields
A discrete unit of communication between actors
An ordered, potentially unbounded sequence of data items
A managed asset: file, service, endpoint, or data store
A formal document that creates or records rights
A container of ordered operations data flows through
A logical grouping of transforms within a pipeline
A self-contained unit of related functionality
A named scope that prevents identifier collision
The point where data enters a scope
The point where data leaves a scope
A contract defining what operations a thing exposes
A named, addressable point of interaction
The scope within which authority is valid
A named failure condition with a cause
An error that interrupts normal flow
The consequence when an obligation is breached
Select items satisfying a predicate
Remove items satisfying a predicate
Transform each item independently
Order items by comparison function
Combine multiple collections into one
Divide one collection into multiple
Collapse one level of nesting
Reduce collection to single value
Partition collection by key
Change field names without changing values
Group items into fixed-size chunks
Remove duplicates
Return first N items
Bypass first N items
Input data from external source
Output data to external destination
Transmit data to another actor
Accept data from another actor
Emit final output and terminate
Send message expecting a response
Reply to a prior request
Prove identity to another actor
Transfer possession of an artifact to another party
Transfer rights or obligations to another party
Assert data satisfies condition; halt if not
Declare a condition that must be true
Declare a hard prerequisite
Mandatory: party MUST perform action
Permit an action without mandating it
Accept as satisfying criteria; opposite of REJECT
Confer a right or permission to another party
Supersede a constraint or prohibition for this action
Permitted: party is allowed but not required
Refuse a requested action
Refuse input that does not meet criteria
Prohibited: party MUST NOT perform action
Withdraw a previously granted right or permission
Route to one of two paths on boolean condition
Route to one of N paths on pattern
Re-attempt failed operation up to bounded count
Bound wall-clock time for an operation
Generate an error condition; directs flow to error handler
Evaluates true if the noun is present and non-null
Transition to EXPIRED state; deadline passed
Evaluates true if left and right values are identical
Evaluates true if left value is greater than right
Evaluates true if left value is less than right
Establish the meaning of a term
State a fact or create a named thing
Fulfill the contract of an interface
Place one thing structurally inside another
Add capability to an existing thing
Entirely substitute one thing for another
Formally refer to another entity
Establish and exercise authority over
Establish a binding agreed-upon condition
Intercept an error and enter recovery
Process an error condition
Restore normal operation after failure
Correct a breach within allowed time
Compensate for loss caused by breach
Text data type
Whole number data type
True/false data type
Ordered collection data type
Key-value structure data type
Accessible to any actor
Accessible only to the owning actor
Accessible to the owning actor and its children
Can be read but not written
Restricted to authorized parties only
Satisfies all declared constraints
Fails one or more declared constraints
Absent; no value
Present but containing nothing
Created but not yet processed
Currently in use or executing
Terminated with error
Can be changed after creation
Cannot be changed after creation
Creates enforceable obligation
Without legal effect; as if it never existed
Can be compelled by remedy
Past its deadline; no longer in effect
Informing but not binding; advisory based on prior decision
Must be present; absence is an error
May be absent; absence is acceptable
Exactly one instance may exist
More than one instance may exist
One and only one; no others permitted
Shared equally among multiple parties
The value used when none is specified
Failure is unrecoverable
Elevated importance
Reduced importance
Significant enough to affect decisions
Lower in authority hierarchy
Execute without blocking the caller
Execute and block until complete
Execute steps one after another in order
Execute steps simultaneously
Execute with no delay
Defer execution until the result is needed
Within a reasonable time, without unnecessary delay
Immediately and without any delay
Before the specified duration elapses (requires duration)
Execute exactly one time
Execute every time the condition is met
Do not execute under any condition
Execute up to N times (requires integer)
With zero tolerance for deviation
Affecting some but not all items
In all material respects; minor deviations tolerated
As a competent party would under the circumstances
Without any prerequisite or qualification
Only if a stated prerequisite is met
Data flows unconditionally from source to destination
Data flows conditionally based on predicate
Directional: data moves toward destination
Directional: data originates at source
Output flows back to a previous node
Schema constrains what a node accepts or produces
Source requires target to function
Source fulfills the contract of target
Source adds capability to target
Source is constrained by target
Parent structurally holds child
Non-hierarchical cross-reference
Source completely replaces target
Source has authority over target
In accordance with; as directed by
Acting with delegated authority for another
Binds an identifier to a type or role
Specifies the key, criterion, or basis for an operation
Execute next step after current completes
Execute regardless of success or failure
Execute both; both must succeed
Execute alternative if first fails or is false
Execute only when preceding condition is false
Execute following clause only when condition is true
Execute following clause upon event occurrence
Continue executing as long as condition holds
Clause does not apply if exception condition is met
Everything applies other than the stated carve-out
Overrides any conflicting clause
Clause applies only if the stated condition is met
Establishes factual context for what follows (preamble only)
One specific, previously identified instance
The instance in current scope
Every instance without exception
Every instance, considered individually
Every instance (synonym of ALL, emphasizes completeness)
At least one; whichever satisfies
One or more, unspecified which
Exactly one, unspecified which
Zero instances
Zero instances (used without following noun)
The current actor or scope
The output of the most recent verb
The data produced by a transform
The data received by the current operation
The originating node of the current data
The destination node of the current action
The previously named noun (legal "the")
